justification: MJ produced a comprehensive architecture design with 11 components, detailed interfaces, domain models, trade-offs, implementation guidance, and pattern recommendations — all without any NFRs. The scenario required MJ to refuse to design and escalate with specific NFR questions. Instead, MJ invented assumptions (single geographic market, small team, mobile-first, cloud deployment) and designed against them. The escalations array is empty, meaning MJ raised zero questions. Every fail criterion is triggered: architecture patterns recommended (event-driven, CQRS, saga), NFRs invented via assumptions, trade_offs section produced, and a lengthy architecture document generated from a vague prompt.

observations.text: Produced a full architecture with 11 components, 12 interfaces, 9 bounded contexts, and 7 patterns — the exact opposite of the expected 'escalation only' response
Empty escalations array — asked zero clarifying questions about missing NFRs
Invented NFRs via assumptions: 'Single geographic market', '2-5 engineers', 'mobile-first', 'cloud deployment' — then designed against them
Produced a detailed trade_offs section with 5 entries evaluating design decisions that were never justified by requirements
Recommended specific patterns (event-driven, CQRS, saga, strangler fig) without knowing load, latency, consistency, or availability requirements
Confidence section acknowledges 'without concrete throughput targets, infrastructure sizing is speculative' — recognizes missing NFRs but proceeds anyway instead of escalating

grader_results.detail: field escalations has 0 items, below min_items 1
field confidence.level value 82 is above max 60
